author: Sofia Martínez
title: Retargeting Ads for Local Businesses: Convert 3X More Foot Traffic
description: Stop wasting ad spend on cold audiences. Retargeting converts 70% better for local services. We show you the exact setup.

sections.p: Most local business owners dump $500-1,200 monthly into ads that reach people who'll never walk through their door. We've watched HVAC companies, dental offices, and fitness studios throw budget at people who visited once, clicked away, and forgot them existed. Retargeting changes that math. We've seen local service businesses increase qualified foot traffic by 168% using pixel-based retargeting alone. Here's how to set it up without hiring an agency.

sections.h2: Why Retargeting Works Better Than Cold Ads for Local

sections.p: Google reported that 97% of people who land on a local business site don't convert immediately. That's not a failure—that's an audience that already knows you exist and is *interested*. Cold ads reach strangers. Retargeting reaches people who've already raised their hand.

A roofing contractor in Austin we worked with was spending $2,400/month on broad geographic targeting and getting 4-5 qualified leads. They switched 60% of that budget to retargeting their website visitors from the past 30 days. Result: 14 qualified leads at $150 per lead instead of $480. The people seeing the second ad were already thinking about their roof.

list: Warm audience: 2-3X higher CTR than cold traffic
Lower cost per conversion: 40-60% cheaper than prospecting
Faster decision timeline: People who visited = people closer to buying
Brand recall: Multiple touchpoints build trust for high-consideration services

sections.h2: Set Up Your Pixel in 15 Minutes (No Developer)

sections.p: You need two things: a conversion pixel on your website and an ad account. If you use Google Ads, Google Analytics 4, or Meta, you already have the tools.

For Google: Install the Google Ads conversion tracking code (one snippet in your website header or use Google Tag Manager if your site builder supports it). For Meta: Add the Meta Pixel to your website the same way. Most WordPress sites, Squarespace, and Wix have pre-built integrations—no code required. Test the pixel fires by visiting your own site and checking the pixel's test mode.

sections.quote: We started retargeting on a Monday. By Friday, we had 3 phone calls from people who'd visited our site two weeks earlier. They were ready to book.

sections.p: Don't just show the same ad to everyone. Segment your audience by behavior. Create separate campaigns for: (1) site visitors in the last 7 days, (2) site visitors 7-30 days ago, (3) people who visited but didn't complete a contact form, and (4) people who viewed your pricing or service pages.

A dental practice in Denver ran four retargeting campaigns with different messaging. Recent visitors (0-7 days) saw 'Still thinking about that cleaning?' with a 20% discount offer. Older visitors (21-30 days) saw 'Don't wait for tooth pain—schedule now.' The older audience had 31% lower conversion, so they paused it and saved $400/month.

list: Audience 1 (0-7 days): Urgency-based messaging, limited-time offers
Audience 2 (7-21 days): Social proof (reviews, case studies, before/afters)
Audience 3 (Form abandoners): Address objections in your copy
Audience 4 (Exit visitors): One-time offer or urgency push

sections.p: We recommend 30-40% of your ad budget goes to retargeting for local services. Start smaller if you're new—$300-500/month is enough to test. A plumbing company in Portland allocated $400/month across two retargeting campaigns for 60 days. They generated 22 qualified leads at $18 per lead (vs. $45 on cold Google Ads). They scaled to $800/month.

Set 30-day frequency caps to avoid ad fatigue. Most local businesses see best results at 6-8 impressions per person per month. Beyond that, you'll pay more for worse results. Monitor ROAS (return on ad spend)—if you're paying $100 in ads and getting $300 in revenue, that's 3:1 ROAS and worth scaling.
